questions.questionText: What is the sum of angles on a straight line?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line always add up to 180 degrees. This is a fundamental property of straight lines in geometry.

questions.questionText: If two angles are vertically opposite, what can be said about their size?
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are formed when two straight lines intersect. A key property is that these angles are always equal in size.

questions.questionText: A triangle has angles 50°, 70°, and x°. Find the value of x.
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in any triangle is 180°. So, 50° + 70° + x° = 180°. This means 120° + x° = 180°, and $x = 180$° - 120° = 60°.

questions.questionText: What type of triangle has two sides of equal length?
questions.explanation: An isosceles triangle is defined as having at least two sides of equal length. Consequently, the angles opposite these equal sides are also equal.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, one angle is 90°. If another angle is 35°, what is the size of the third angle?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So, 90° + 35° + x° = 180°. This simplifies to 125° + x° = 180°, which means $x = 180$° - 125° = 55°.

questions.questionText: Which type of angle is greater than 90° but less than 180°?
questions.explanation: An obtuse angle is an angle whose measure is greater than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees. An acute angle is less than 90°, a right angle is exactly 90°, and a reflex angle is greater than 180°.

questions.questionText: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal. If an interior angle on one side is 110°, what is the size of the other interior angle on the same side?
questions.explanation: Co-interior (or consecutive interior) angles formed by a transversal intersecting parallel lines add up to 180°. So, 180° - 110° = 70°.

questions.questionText: What is an angle that measures exactly 90° called?
questions.explanation: An angle that measures exactly 90° is known as a right angle. It is often indicated by a small square symbol at the vertex.

questions.questionText: If two angles add up to 90°, they are called:
questions.explanation: Complementary angles are two angles whose sum is exactly 90°. Supplementary angles add up to 180°.

questions.questionText: An equilateral triangle has one angle measuring 60°. What are the measures of the other two angles?
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has all three sides of equal length and all three angles equal. Since the sum of angles in a triangle is 180°, each angle must be 180° / 3 = 60°.

questions.questionText: In the diagram, angle A is 40° and angle B is an alternate interior angle to angle A. What is the measure of angle B?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal when two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. Therefore, if angle A is 40°, angle B is also 40°.

questions.questionText: What is the sum of the exterior angles of any convex polygon?
options: Depends on the number of sides
questions.explanation: The sum of the exterior angles of any convex polygon, regardless of the number of sides, is always 360°. This is a key property of polygons.
